spec_match.rationale: Incredibly clear visual comparison of partitioned storage keys (top-site, embedded-origin) versus unpartitioned shared access keys.

interactive.rationale: Provides clickable tab controls that immediately swap diagrams and textual insights between Chrome 150 and pre-150 architectures.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Perfectly aligned with the parent index: lets users toggle models, showing visual storage buckets and key isolation state.

edge_cases.rationale: Features clear explanations of cross-site identity linking risks under the old architecture.

references.rationale: The page contains absolutely no see-also references to external specifications or databases. It fails the repository-wide hard rule: 'Every feature and concept page must include a chromestatus.com/feature/<id> link'.

design_system.rationale: Clean borders, custom legend dots, card alignments, and crumbs complying with the shared design system.

openQuestions.title: Add mandatory ChromeStatus entry link and standard see-also footer
openQuestions.detail: To comply with the repository-wide hard rule ('Every feature and concept page must include a chromestatus.com/feature/<id> link'), we must add a 'see also' footer section containing a link to ChromeStatus entry 5162221567082496, alongside references to the MDN partitioned cookies page.

summary: A beautiful, highly educational visualizer comparing partitioned storage structures to old cross-site tracking setups. However, it currently fails the repository's hard rule by omitting a direct link to the ChromeStatus feature database. Adding a standard see-also section with this link is a high priority.
